headline: Bounded JSON and safe URL parsing on the sync WebSocket channel
summary: The sync WebSocket channel now decodes incoming frames with a size- and depth-limited JSON parser and parses the upgrade request-target through the hardened URL parser. Error responses on the remaining stash and bundle service paths move to the RFC 9457 problem-details shape.

items.title: Decode sync WebSocket frames with bounded JSON
items.body: Messages on the /sync/ws channel are now parsed with a size- and nesting-limited JSON decoder, so an oversized or deeply nested frame is rejected with an error reply rather than parsed unbounded. The existing per-message size limit is unchanged; this adds an independent depth bound.

items.title: Parse the WebSocket upgrade target with the hardened URL parser
items.body: The /sync/ws upgrade request-target is now parsed through b.safeUrl, which applies a length cap and a protocol allowlist, replacing the deprecated legacy URL parser. A malformed or over-long upgrade target is dropped at the handshake.

items.title: Return RFC 9457 problem-details on the remaining stash and bundle error paths
items.body: The access-code verification, stash finalize, and sync file rename/delete responses now return application/problem+json with { type, title, status, detail } instead of the legacy { error } body, matching the rest of the API. The HTTP status is unchanged; read .detail with .error as a fallback for older servers.
